name: Platform Adaptation Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Reduced dependency on Apple/Google → Systemic: Increased resilience through open standards → Strategic: Enhanced digital sovereignty.
options: Implement FIDO2/WebAuthn and OpenID Connect on GrapheneOS to reduce dependence on mobile OS duopoly.
Develop a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication path to increase platform neutrality.
Establish a certified fallback access path for critical public authentication through EU bodies and technical working groups.
review: Controls Platform Lock-in vs. Supplier Concentration Risk. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of implementing open standards in existing infrastructure.

name: Digital Identity Standards
consequences: Immediate: Alignment with eIDAS and NSIS → Systemic: Increased adoption through procurement pressure → Strategic: Improved digital identity resilience.
options: Develop a set of standards for platform-neutral and fallback-capable access models to influence AltID and future procurement requirements.
review: Controls Procurement Process vs. Technical Feasibility. Weakness: The option fails to consider the potential resistance from existing operators.

name: Coalition Building
consequences: Immediate: Increased stakeholder engagement → Systemic: Broader coalition support → Strategic: Enhanced digital identity resilience.
options: Build a coalition spanning civil-society organisations, privacy advocates, academic researchers, security experts, and sympathetic members of Folketinget's digital policy committee.
review: Controls Stakeholder Engagement vs. Regulatory Pressure. Weakness: The option fails to consider the potential influence of regulatory bodies on coalition building.

name: Procurement Intervention
consequences: Immediate: Inclusion of platform-neutrality and fallback access language in procurement documents → Systemic: Increased adoption through procurement pressure → Strategic: Improved digital identity resilience.
options: Influence AltID-related design, policy, consultation, or procurement documents to include requirements for platform-neutral and fallback-capable access models.
review: Controls Procurement Process vs. Technical Feasibility. Weakness: The option fails to consider the potential resistance from existing operators.

name: Regulatory Engagement
consequences: Immediate: Alignment with eIDAS and NSIS → Systemic: Increased adoption through regulatory pressure → Strategic: Improved digital identity resilience.
options: Engage with EU bodies and technical working groups connected to the European Digital Identity Wallet framework to align Danish advocacy with broader European requirements.
review: Controls Regulatory Pressure vs. Stakeholder Engagement. Weakness: The option fails to consider the potential influence of stakeholder engagement on regulatory pressure.

name: Identity Ecosystem Redesign
consequences: Immediate: Increased platform neutrality → Systemic: Reduced supplier concentration risk → Strategic: Enhanced digital sovereignty.
options: Implement a decentralized identity management system to reduce dependence on Apple/Google.
review: Controls Identity Management vs. Supplier Concentration Risk. Weakness: The option fails to consider the potential impact on existing user habits and trust models.

name: Digital Trust Framework
consequences: Immediate: Alignment with eIDAS and NSIS → Systemic: Increased adoption through regulatory pressure → Strategic: Improved digital identity resilience.
options: Develop a digital trust framework that prioritizes platform neutrality and fallback-capable access models.
review: Controls Regulatory Pressure vs. Stakeholder Engagement. Weakness: The option fails to consider the potential influence of stakeholder engagement on regulatory pressure.

consequences: Immediate: Increased adoption through open standards → Systemic: Reduced supplier concentration risk → Strategic: Enhanced digital sovereignty.

review: Controls Authentication Protocol vs. Supplier Concentration Risk. Weakness: The option fails to consider the potential impact on existing infrastructure and user habits.
